What teachers other school officials reduce spread influenza?
Recognize symptoms flu.
symptoms fever 100°F 37.8°C), cough, sore throat, body aches, headache, chills fatigue.
Sometimes diarrhea vomiting occur; however, these symptoms usually main problem.
Prevent others from becoming sick.
Children with symptoms (see above) should removed from classroom soon possible.
Send child school nurse designated school official.
Keep children apart from others while waiting someone take them home.
Contain persons.
Designate area where children stay while waiting leave school.
This area should away from common rooms areas where others could exposed.
Designate staff watch persons until they sent home.
Limit number people involved.
Designated staff should increased risk influenza-related complications (i.e., pregnant individuals, immunocompromised persons, persons with chronic health conditions, etc.).
Follow exclusion guidelines.
Inform parent/guardian that child excluded from school school activities (sports activities, clubs, dances, etc.)
until least hours after their fever gone without using fever-reducing medications, even child taking antiviral medication.

vaccine every year.
single best protect against seasonal children staff seasonal influenza vaccine each year.
vaccination recommended children aged months older.
Wash your hands.
Wash your hands several times using soap warm water 15-20 seconds.
Check restrooms regularly ensure that soap paper towels always available.
Alcohol-based hand sanitizers also effective.
alcohol-based hand sanitizers.
Alcohol-based hand gels used classrooms minimize disruption.
Hand sanitizer with least alcohol effective killing germs hands when they visibly soiled.
Appropriate times practice good hand hygiene after coughing, sneezing, contact with infected surfaces (i.e., desks, doorknobs).
Cover your mouth nose with tissue when cough sneeze.
Make sure tissues available classrooms.
Tissues should thrown away immediately, then followed cleaning your hands.
don't have tissue, cough sneeze into your elbow shoulder; into your hands.
Avoid touching your eyes, nose, mouth.
Germs spread this way.
Stay home when ill. student, teacher, staff member reporting flu-like symptoms should stay home sent home until least hours after their fever gone without fever-reducing medications, even individual taking antiviral medication.
Clean surfaces frequently.
school, clean commonly used surfaces such door handles, handrails, eating surfaces, desks, etc., frequently with detergent-based cleaners registered disinfectants that normally used school setting.
Special cleaning products needed.
cleaning products according directions product label.
bleach solutions, chlorine bleach with gallon cool water.
Bleach solutions should changed daily.
Additional, extensive cleaning school settings wiping down floors walls necessary this been demonstrated decrease spread influenza.
Remember school bus.
Clean commonly handled interior surfaces (i.e., door handles, hand rails, etc.)
between groups students.

Report high absentee rates your local health department.
Your health department will work with help stop spread illness.

what does this mean about
This means that currently feel most energised when around other people socialising.
It's biggest change think because before EXTREMELY introverted (ie.
felt most energised when alone having time myself, felt drained energy when being around others).
this time life now, have larger friend group enjoy seeing much can, feel happiest when around lots other people crowds completely different before!
This stayed same; intuition abstract patterns, possibilities make decisions (rather than logic concrete evidence).
Also stayed same; when making decisions doing things peoples' emotions feelings priority rather than rationality.
This change too!
means have become more spontaneous flexible adaptable change suppose I've kind been forced into it!)
rather than 'Judging' before, which meant preferred structure, planning routine.
always advocate learning about what makes other people around tick, highly recommend taking this test haven't already!
